steps.title: Recognize the most effective introduction strategy
steps.content: To present a concept to an unfamiliar audience, the most effective tool is a clear definition that explains what it is and what distinguishes it. Examples, contrasts, or topic shifts come later; definitions come first.

steps.title: Confirm with the choices
steps.content: Choice A provides the definition: historical analysis is an approach to art criticism that considers the historical context in which a work was created. Choices B, C, and D offer an example, switch to stylistic analysis, or contrast approaches without defining historical analysis itself. That matches choice A — A is correct.
